metadata.name: rammed_earth_shelter
metadata.title: Rammed Earth Shelter
metadata.description: Emergency housing construction tutorial - build a shelter from scratch using rammed earth technique and PDF guides.
metadata.author: uDOS

quests.title: Build Emergency Shelter
quests.description: Construct a basic rammed earth shelter for protection from elements

events.text:
=== RAMMED EARTH SHELTER TUTORIAL ===

Shelter is your second priority after water. The 'Rule of Threes' says you can die in 3 hours without shelter in extreme conditions.

Today you'll learn to build emergency housing using rammed earth - one of humanity's oldest building techniques.

RAMMED EARTH BASICS:

Rammed earth uses:
- Soil (clay + sand + gravel mix)
- Temporary wooden forms
- Manual or pneumatic tamper
- No cement (optional stabilizer)

Advantages:
- Free materials from site
- Excellent thermal mass
- Fire resistant
- Durable (lasts centuries)
- No special tools needed

events.prompt: What type of shelter do you build?

events.text:
=== STEP 1: SITE PREPARATION ===

Choose your location wisely:
- Elevated ground (drainage)
- Away from flood zones
- Near water source
- South-facing (northern hemisphere)
- Protected from wind
- Access to materials
You clear the site and level the ground. A proper foundation prevents water infiltration and structural issues.

=== STEP 2: SOIL TESTING ===

The JAR TEST:
1. Fill jar 1/3 with soil
2. Add water to fill jar
3. Shake vigorously
4. Let settle for 24 hours
5. Observe layers:
   - Bottom: Gravel/sand
   - Middle: Silt
   - Top: Clay

Ideal mix: 70% sand/gravel, 30% clay
Your soil test results: Good balance of clay and sand. This will compact well and provide strength.

=== STEP 3: BUILD FORMS ===

You need temporary wooden forms to contain the earth while ramming.
You construct simple wooden forms: two parallel boards held 12-18 inches apart. These will create walls 12-24 inches thick - excellent insulation and thermal mass.

=== STEP 4: RAMMING ===

This is the core technique:

RAMMING PROCESS:
1. Slightly moisten soil (squeeze test: holds shape, doesn't drip)
2. Fill form with 4-6 inches of soil
3. Tamp with wooden ram or pneumatic tamper
4. Compact to 50% original height
5. Repeat layers until wall height reached
6. Move forms up, continue

The more you ram, the stronger the wall.

events.prompt: How thoroughly do you ram the earth?

if_true.text: You take your time, compacting each layer until it's rock-hard. This produces walls that will last for generations.
You maintain good compaction while keeping a steady pace. The walls are solid and will serve well.
You work quickly, but the compaction isn't ideal. The shelter will work for emergency use but may not last as long.

events.text:
=== STEP 5: ROOF ===

Rammed earth walls are strong but need protection from rain.

ROOFING OPTIONS:
1. Thatched roof (traditional, renewable)
2. Metal sheets (durable, waterproof)
3. Living roof (insulated, beautiful)
4. Tarp + earth (emergency, simple)

Key: OVERHANG - extend roof 2+ feet past walls to protect from rain.
You construct a simple sloped roof with metal sheeting. The wide overhang protects your walls from erosion.

You add:
- Door frame and simple door
- Small window for ventilation
- Drainage around foundation
- Interior earth floor (compacted)

Your shelter is complete!

=== KNOWLEDGE SUMMARY ===

You've learned:
- Site selection principles
- Soil testing and composition
- Form construction
- Ramming technique
- Roof weatherproofing
- Foundation drainage

This shelter will protect you from elements, provides thermal mass for temperature regulation, and cost almost nothing to build.

Rammed earth shelters have stood for thousands of years. Now you can build one anywhere.

events.message:
=== SHELTER BUILDING COMPLETE ===

You've constructed a durable rammed earth shelter using ancient techniques and local materials.

Skill tree unlocked: SHELTER

This knowledge is now yours forever. In any survival scenario, you can build protection from the elements.
